original_abstract: This paper provides a comprehensive comparative analysis of DeepSeek-R1 and OpenAI’s ChatGPT, examining the architectural, methodological, and operational differences between these two prominent Large Language Models (LLMs). While ChatGPT follows a proprietary, dense transformer-based approach refined through Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) and Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF), DeepSeek-R1 introduces a cost-efficient alternative utilizing a Mixture of Experts (MoE) architecture and Multi-Head Latent Attention (MLA). Notably, DeepSeek-R1 emphasizes a multi-stage training process heavily grounded in rule-based reinforcement learning, which bolsters its performance in specialized reasoning, mathematical, and technical tasks. In contrast, ChatGPT maintains a competitive edge in general-purpose conversation, linguistic fluency, and creative adaptability. This study evaluates both models across several dimensions, including benchmark performance, transparency, ethical considerations, and data privacy. By synthesizing their respective strengths and limitations, the paper highlights the trade-offs between open-source customization and closed-source user experience. Ultimately, the findings suggest that while neither model dominates every domain, the choice between them is dictated by specific organizational needs, such as computational budget, transparency requirements, and the complexity of the target reasoning tasks. The paper concludes by addressing broader AI challenges, including data quality, algorithmic "black box" problems, and the evolving regulatory landscape.
humanized_abstract: The paper presents a thorough comparative study of DeepSeek-R1 and the widely used ChatGPT from OpenAI. It highlights their structural, design and operational differences and argues that the choice between them or future variants depends on the concrete needs of the organizations that will eventually adopt them. While ChatGPT leverages the same proprietary technology as other models from OpenAI such as the dense transformer and the supervised fine-tuned (SFT) and reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF) that enhance its language abilities, DeepSeek-R1 leverages a mixture of experts architecture and multi-head latent attention (MLA) to achieve an optimal level of performance at a reasonable computational cost. The multi-stage training mechanism and the prevalence of the rule-based reinforcement learning in each stage give DeepSeek-R1 an extra bonus for specialized reasoning, math and technology tasks. However, for everyday conversation, natural language understanding and creativeness, ChatGPT is a more competitive model. We test both models in various aspects including performances, explainability, ethical and data protection. Our overall analysis sums up the pros and cons of using an open-source model as DeepSeek-R1 or a commercial such as ChatGPT. The conclusion remarks on the future AI challenges related to data quality, black boxes and legislation.
